item.title: Slow Read exhibition asks us to ditch doom-scrolling for art celebrating the printed page
item.description: A new show at Hawthorn Arts Centre offers a range of immersive artworks made from books and paper that demand, and reward, our full attention.

item.title: Proposed amendment to Arapiles Dyurrite plan allows climbing near culturally significant areas
item.description: Climbing Victoria says proposed changes to the Mount Arapiles Dyurrite management plan will be a compromise between climbers and traditional owners built on "trust".

item.title: Gianni Infantino says FIFA will discuss World Cup expansion post-tournament, defends hydration breaks
item.description: FIFA president Gianni Infantino believes this year's World Cup is proof that expanding the tournament was the right move.

item.title: Toowoomba man Dharminder Singh faces court charged with the murder of Jana Armstrong
item.description: Toowoomba man Dharminder Singh, 48, whose former partner, Jana Armstrong's body was discovered on the weekend, has appeared in a Darling Downs court charged with murder.

item.title: Queensland teen dies, 22 injured in e-mobility device crashes since safety laws brought in
item.description: Regulations introduced in Queensland less than a fortnight ago limit e-mobility devices to 12km/h on footpaths. Riders must be 16 and above or be supervised by a parent. They can also be breathalysed.

item.title: Consumer advocates intensify calls for mandatory domestic mobile roaming
item.description: Already available to international tourists, consumer advocates want to see mobile roaming mandated for domestic customers too.

item.title: Family of Melbourne woman Lavanya Chappa pay tribute to 'loving mother'
item.description: Lavanya Chappa has been remembered as a devoted mother and dear friend, one week after the mother-of-two was stabbed to death in her Melbourne home.

item.title: As new strikes rattle the Gulf, Iran's lead negotiator sends a clear message to Washington
item.description: As another round of strikes rattles the Gulf region, Iran's lead negotiator makes it very clear where the regime stands on the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Sentencing of Molly Ticehurst's killer Daniel Billings brought forward
item.description: The sentencing of the man who murdered New South Wales woman Molly Ticehurst has been brought forward to allow a psychiatrist to give evidence.

item.title: Bazball is over as Brendon McCullum's deliberately uncomplicated regime falls foul of itself, leaving complicated legacy
item.description: Brendon McCullum deliberately set out to make England's men's Test team a less complicated team to play cricket for. After four years of Bazball's highs and lows, it leaves a very complicated legacy.

item.title: Thomas Tuchel's exchange with Jude Bellingham gives England another chance to prove its World Cup mettle
item.description: This England World Cup squad is proving they are different to their predecessors at every stop. Now their manager has just given them their latest challenge.

item.title: Payne Haas to stay with Samoa and says nation is 'big chance' to win Rugby League World Cup
item.description: Payne Haas has cleared the air about his World Cup plans and says there is a "great chance" Samoa can lift the trophy for the first time.

item.title: Queensland government defends $40m cut to family violence prevention funding
item.description: The Queensland opposition has labelled the cuts to family and domestic violence funding "indefensible".

item.title: Five of our best chicken dinner recipes
item.description: From pot pie to tray bakes and sticky maple wings, here are five of our most popular chicken recipes.

item.title: Actor Sam Neill, star of Jurassic Park, dies
item.description: New Zealand actor Sam Neill, who starred in My Brilliant Career and Jurassic Park, has died aged 78.

item.title: Queensland wildlife rescuers prepare for bird flu after sick giant petrel found at Noosa
item.description: A northern giant petrel found at Noosa Main Beach is undergoing testing for H5 avian influenza.

item.title: Rescuers retrieve a sick seabird from Noosa Main Beach
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: SA coroner calls for changes after man died while paramedic waited outside
item.description: A paramedic who chose not to force entry to a house, despite being told a man was inside and needed medical attention, had "a flawed understanding" of his powers to enter the property, an inquest finds.

item.title: Actor Sam Neill's final take
item.description: Actor Sam Neill might have officially been a Kiwi but he did some of his best work in Australian film and television. At home on either side of the ditch, this is Sam Neill's story.

item.title: What we know about Christian Jack Joseph, the Perth doctor accused of killing a man in Fremantle
item.description: A young Perth doctor and musician is behind bars, accused of killing a 63-year-old man after a late-night Fremantle scuffle. What do we know?

item.title: Australia has a sperm donor shortage and women are turning to social media and apps
item.description: Australia is facing a sperm donation shortage and it is leading those desperate to find a way to conceive down some expensive and potentially dangerous paths.

item.title: From Jurassic Park to The Piano, here are Sam Neill's best roles
item.description: With more than 150 acting roles under his belt, Sam Neill leaves a lasting legacy to the film and TV industry, and an array of characters that will stay in fans' hearts forever.

item.title: Geelong forward Jeremy Cameron to have AC joint surgery, Hawthorn's Karl Amon has knee procedure
item.description: Geelong are hopeful star forward Jeremy Cameron will return in time to play a part in their premiership push after suffering a brutal shoulder injury.

item.title: Sydney daycare worker facing 329 charges of alleged child abuse identified as Hamish Tait
item.description: A Sydney childcare worker faces more than 320 charges over his alleged abuse of 136 children over a 16-year period, as police reveal a list of locations where he worked.

item.title: Fiery tobacco wars continue in Melbourne
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Unaffordable: Australia's broken housing promise
item.description: Four Corners is the home of Australian investigative journalism.

item.title: Australia facing a sperm donor shortage
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: UK counter-terrorism police now leading Ann Widdecombe murder investigation
item.description: The counter-terrorism unit has taken charge of the case due to "new information and evidence" on the suspected murder of the former minister, the UK Home Secretary says.

item.title: ABC Places quiz: Where in the world are we?
item.description: Your challenge in the ABC Places quiz is to work out the location of 10 photos from around the world. Be warned: we might try to trick you.

item.title: Negotiating your bills could save you thousands. Here's how to start
item.description: Negotiating to reduce your spending and increase your income are tangible ways you can improve your financial situation right now.

item.title: Explain the Internet: Is the controversy over Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey justified?
item.description: The tech trillionaire has spent a good chunk of 2026 yelling on X about how Christopher Nolan's adaptation of Homer's Odyssey "desecrates" the ancient poet.

item.title: Property developers are benefitting from eased planning rules targeting 'affordable' housing
item.description: State and federal governments are offering incentives for property developers in exchange for more affordable housing. But a Four Corners investigation has uncovered major flaws in the strategy.

item.title: Big super funds and SMSFs could pay compensation for victims of financial misconduct
item.description: The assistant treasurer is looking to revamp funding for the Compensation Scheme of Last Resort, saying that large super funds, as well as people with self-managed super funds (SMSFs), could be asked to chip in.

item.title: Muslim man attacked by two men on way to mosque raises alarm on rising hate
item.description: A Muslim man has escaped a near-miss knife attack on his way to evening prayers after police say he was set upon by two men metres away from his local mosque.

item.title: Spielberg leads outpouring of global tributes for actor Sam Neill
item.description: The New Zealand actor died suddenly at the age of 78, with Hollywood directors and actors and prominent political figures all paying tribute to a great of global cinema.

item.title: Safety of women and girls under national spotlight again after four alleged murders in four days
item.description: Experts are calling for more research and better funding for domestic violence prevention after four women and girls were killed in separate incidents last week.

item.title: More than 2,000 sexual extortion complaints spark warning to young men
item.description: Major online platforms are accused of failing to deploy readily available technology to detect well-known blackmail tactics.

item.title: Flames surge violently from Bangkok pub as patrons flee
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Video appears to show Thailand pub fire reached flashover 'point of no return', expert says
item.description: A video analysis of an explosive pub fire in Thailand, which killed 30 people, appears to show a flashover "point of no return" that would have made evacuation nearly impossible, an expert says.

item.title: Wannabe Olympic MMA group tried gifting $82,000 cognac to Brisbane Games committee
item.description: The gifts, revealed exclusively by the ABC today, are part of a wider series of examples of attempted influence-peddling from businesses and sporting bodies before the Games kick off in 2032.

item.title: Four more charged over alleged kidnapping and murder of Chris Baghsarian in Sydney
item.description: A man accused of coordinating the alleged murder and kidnapping of Sydney grandfather Chris Baghsarian has been charged.

item.title: Overseas dealers flock to Winton Opal Festival
item.description: The outback town of Winton is known for dinosaur fossils and as the location of the first performance of Waltzing Matilda, but has also become an important part of a growing international luxury trade.

item.title: What happened to Peter Falconio? The outback murder mystery that continues to haunt Australia 25 years on
item.description: Twenty-five years ago, the murder of British backpacker Peter Falconio and the abduction and escape of his girlfriend, Joanne Lees, in the remote Central Australian outback captured global attention. Today, it remains one of the nation's most infamous cases.

item.title: Former Victorian Young Australian of the Year charged over imitation guns, police uniforms
item.description: Darcy McGauley-Bartlett has been arrested and charged with a raft of offences, including possessing imitation firearms and police uniforms.

item.title: Alex de Minaur notches career-high ranking despite Wimbledon exit
item.description: Alex de Minaur's arrow keeps pointing upwards despite his wait for a grand slam title being lengthened by a disappointing Wimbledon exit.

item.title: Angus Taylor says he criticised One Nation with a 'heavy heart' — as it happened
item.description: The opposition leader says he reluctantly gave a speech criticising the policies of Pauline Hanson's One Nation.

item.title: Oil soars over 10pc on heightened Middle East tensions while ASX down — as it happened
item.description: Oil prices soar more than 10 per cent, with analysts saying they could rise further as global supplies reach critically low levels. The ASX is following Wall Street down as the world digests the news.

item.title: Will property tax changes stem Australia's productivity crisis?
item.description: With housing prices coming off the boil, maybe our banks will start lending to more productive areas of the economy, like business.

item.title: Dutch referee Rob Dieperink dead at 38 two months after World Cup axing
item.description: Dutch football mourns the sudden death of "highly valued referee" Rob Dieperink just months after FIFA removed him from the panel of World Cup officials.

item.title: Entangled humpback whale on Ningaloo Reef
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Five whale entanglements in five days bring sharks, close beaches on Ningaloo Reef
item.description: Authorities save one animal but another succumbs to its injuries, leading sharks to converge on a popular snorkelling spot.

item.title: Sharks feed on whale carcass off Exmouth
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Former Spanish PM slammed over 'intolerable whiffs of racism' towards France team
item.description: Mariano Rajoy finds himself in hot water after saying France's team "does not have any French players" ahead of the World Cup semifinal.

item.title: Cameron Munster may miss Melbourne Storm's NRL finals due to knee surgery
item.description: Cameron Munster goes under the knife for knee surgery, likely ruling him out of action for the majority of regular season.

item.title: World Cup: Spain 'here to suffer' and France unites against racism ahead of semifinal
item.description: Spain is invoking the aura of the Roman Empire, while France is lighting up the pitch and remaining united off it, ahead of their men's World Cup semi-final in Dallas.

item.title: Australia's lamb export gains ground in US as protein 'wave' creates demand
item.description: Australians may love their lamb, but it has always been a harder sell for American consumers. There are signs that is changing.

item.title: Death of former AFL player Paul Atkins shocks Tasmania's sporting community
item.description: A former AFL player has been remembered for his larger-than-life personality after being hit by a car and killed on Saturday night in Tasmania.

item.title: The four stages of the mental load
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: 'I lost a normal childhood': What it's like being a young carer
item.description: Kenny and Naomi were both younger than 10 when they became carers for family members.

item.title: Police seize vehicle but yet to find driver after fatal Logan hit-and-run
item.description: A 20-year-old pedestrian died after he was struck on Middle Road at Greenbank shortly before 9pm.

item.title: Considerable spike in oil price could spell higher petrol prices for motorists
item.description: The recent closure of the Strait of Hormuz saw the oil price lift, but buying in the market accelerated following Donald Trump's latest Truth Social post.

item.title: Peter Falconio's body could be 70km from murder scene, former police officer says
item.description: Jim Whitehead has helped to close some of Australia's most high-profile missing persons cases, including those of Daniel Morcombe and Allison Baden-Clay. Now he is looking to help find the remains of British backpacker Peter Falconio.

item.title: Bird flu detection training to be rolled out to SA conservation volunteers
item.description: Training to help volunteers identify potential cases of bird flu will be rolled out in South Australia, under a partnership between conservation groups and government.

item.title: Authorities seize nearly $10 million worth of illegal tobacco and vapes in Tasmania
item.description: Tasmanian authorities seized five times as many cigarettes and three times as many vapes in the last financial year as they did the year prior. Enforcement agencies have been given greater powers, including the ability to shut down stores selling illegal tobacco.

item.title: Gold Coast Suns extend Jed Walter contract, Western Bulldogs suffer Bailey Dale injury blow
item.description: Shunning big-money offers from rival clubs, Jed Walter said the lure of achieving AFL glory with his childhood club made re-signing a "pretty easy decision".

item.title: Sydney radio ratings reveal KIIS on the slide without Kyle and Jackie O
item.description: The KIIS Sydney audience has slipped further in the radio ratings in the latest blow to ARN after the departure of renowned breakfast radio juggernaut Kyle and Jackie O.

item.title: Jarome Luai joins Parramatta, Benji Marshall slams relationship breakdown rumours
item.description: Benji Marshall has slammed "bull**** rumours" claiming a fractured relationship with Jarome Luai contributed to the star five-eighth's early exit from Wests Tigers.

item.title: Mystery surrounds sudden departure of Mildura Base Public Hospital chief executive
item.description: Mystery surrounds the departure of the chief executive of Mildura Base Public Hospital, who led the north-west Victorian hospital through its transition from private to public.

item.title: Major SA highways given lowest possible safety rating by Australian Road Assessment Program
item.description: Parts of the Sturt Highway, which connects Sydney to Adelaide and provides an integral part of the country's freight network, have been given the lowest possible safety rating by the Australian Road Assessment Program (AusRAP).

item.title: Cyber warning over Russian hackers targeting critical industries
item.description: The Australian Signals Directorate says hackers are trying to access information through network devices, such as laptops, which use old software and predictable passwords.

item.title: Sentencing delayed for Jatin Pudaruth over assault of 87yo Rossmoyne traffic warden
item.description: An 18-year-old assaulted an 87-year-old traffic warden after he subjected the young man's girlfriend to "persistent unwanted attention", a Perth court hears.

item.title: Racism remains a key issue for AFL players while Indigenous AFLW players rate workplace culture low
item.description: Racism remains a key issue for First Nations AFL players, while Indigenous AFLW players rate their workplace culture significantly lower than their peers, a new report finds.

item.title: Woman strangled by wheelchair lap belt due to staff shortage, court hears
item.description: A woman who died after slipping from her wheelchair and being strangled by its lap belt had been left unsupervised due to a staff shortage at an Adelaide care facility, a court has heard.

item.title: Man charged with murder over weekend death of 58-year-old Morwell man
item.description: A 43-year-old man has been charged with murder following the discovery of the body of a 58-year-old Morwell man in Victoria's east at the weekend.

item.title: Family of motorcycle crash victim calls for careless driver to face prison time
item.description: The family of a beloved member of the Northern Territory's motorsport community has urged a court to send a woman who failed to give way to him to jail over the fatal crash.

item.title: Giant petrel found on Noosa Main Beach tests negative for H5 bird flu
item.description: The giant petrel was taken to RSPCA Queensland's Wildlife Rehabilitation Centre where it underwent testing by Biosecurity Queensland as part of its surveillance program.

item.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: PBS access to two vital MS treatments under threat
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Launceston General Hospital experiencing 'impacted' capacity due to high demand
item.description: Tasmania's top health official says it will take the Launceston General Hospital "two or three days" to bring its services back to an acceptable level, after it issued a notice urging patients to only attend if it is a medical emergency.

item.title: Anthony Albanese maps out AI future with new national framework
item.description: Labor will develop a new national framework in response to artificial intelligence as it seeks to address the technology’s growing economic and social impacts.

item.title: Buffett severs donations to Gates Foundation following Epstein revelations
item.description: The American investor will end his longtime donations to the Microsoft co-founder's non-profit.

item.title: Donald Trump ditches plan for fee in Strait of Hormuz as Iran and US trade new strikes
item.description: Donald Trump reneges on his pledge to charge a 20 per cent fee on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, a day after saying he would introduce it.

item.title: Spain soars into World Cup final with stunning upset over favourite France
item.description: Spain is through to the World Cup final thanks to a 2-0 win over France.

item.title: Impressionism's legacy reconsidered in exhibitions at AGSA and Geelong Gallery
item.description: More than 150 years after the first Impressionist exhibition in Paris, the Art Gallery of South Australia and Geelong Gallery cast new light on the influential art movement.

item.title: Art class uses creativity to paint different picture of mental health
item.description: In regional NSW, one program shows that creativity may be the key to better mental health.

item.title: Travel quiz, July 15: Dust off your passport and join us on a trip
item.description: Which of these peaks isn't in the Himalayas? And in what country would you find the island of Rapa Nui?

item.title: The Thai child sex exploitation scourge unfolding at bars, restaurants and hotels
item.description: Thailand is struggling against a "hidden" world of child sexual exploitation that requires action from both the tourism industry and law enforcement agencies, according to NGOs.

item.title: Batteries 'cheaper' than gas plants as data centres fuel turbine costs
item.description: Falling battery costs and a data centre-fuelled dash for gas have overturned decades of accepted wisdom: gas is no longer the cheapest way to meet peak power demand.

item.title: Japan ramps up defences amid concerns about China's 'military activities'
item.description: While the world has been watching the rise of China, Japan has been in the middle of its biggest defence shift since World War II.

item.title: Telstra compensation process puts onus on customers, consumer body says
item.description: Telstra is assessing outage compensation claims, but consumer advocates say the process is cumbersome and puts the onus on customers rather than the telco at fault.

item.title: Australia's best-paid executive lives in US and earns 500 times that of average full-time worker
item.description: Australia's most highly paid executive collected a salary nearly 500 times that of the country's full-time average worker.

item.title: What would Australia's economy look like under One Nation?
item.description: From interest rates to policy advice from billionaire buddy Gina Rinehart, here is how One Nation's economic platform could look.

item.title: US, Iran exchange fire as Trump issues new threats — as it happened
item.description: US President Donald Trump says he will not charge a 20 per cent toll on traffic in the Strait of Hormuz, as the US military confirms its blockade of Iran has come into effect.

item.title: Counterterrorism police treating suspected murder of Ann Widdecombe as a targeted attack
item.description: After initially saying the alleged murder of British former politician Ann Widdecombe was not politically motivated, police now say they believe it was a targeted attack.

item.title: Fertiliser company Neutrog fined over smelly manure piles at Kanmantoo
item.description: An Adelaide Hills business that piled chicken manure, coffee grounds and other waste up to twice the allowed height has been fined by a South Australian court after more than 700 complaints about the smell.

item.title: New owner of Rushy Lagoon outlines plans for pine, cattle and tourism
item.description: The buyer of Tasmania's largest farm says in addition to the pine plantation it has planned for the property, it's also considering cropping and leasing out parts of the farm for grazing, tourism and mountain biking.

item.title: 'People want community': Hobby-based clubs tackling loneliness
item.description: Hobby-based pop-up clubs are attracting people from university students and office workers to new migrants and academics.

item.title: Two people killed in truck crash at Heathcote, north of Melbourne
item.description: A car and truck collided about 9.45pm on the McIvor Highway at Heathcote.

item.title: Tadej Pogačar wins stage 10 to extend Tour de France lead over rivals
item.description: Tadej Pogačar takes another step towards a fifth Tour de France win with a breakaway stage victory, with some fans booing him because of his dominance.

item.title: AI-powered glasses improve everyday life for disability sector amid privacy concerns
item.description: For people who are blind, AI-powered smart glasses deliver a level of independence that was once out of reach, but the rise of wearable cameras also raises urgent questions about surveillance, consent and the law.

item.title: Kakadu's closed waterfalls spark stern warning from NT tourism minister
item.description: It is known as the jewel in the Northern Territory's tourism crown, but visitors to Kakadu National Park are being greeted by closure signs blocking roads to key waterfall sites.

item.title: Labor to force data centres to minimise water, underwrite own power — as it happened
item.description: The PM has announced new artificial intelligence data centres will be forced to underwrite or supply their own power and minimise their use of water under new AI standards to be introduced to parliament.

item.title: Australia grapples with child psychiatrist shortage as experts warn of consequences
item.description: Experts are warning younger children are presenting with increasingly complex mental health needs as a new report predicts a nationwide shortfall of child and adolescent psychiatrists.

item.title: NRLW players hope NRL broadcast deal brings longer season, full-time salaries
item.description: A $5.3 billion broadcast deal, booming participation and new contracts on the horizon have NRLW stars hopeful their league can soar to new heights.

item.title: ASX rises after US reimposes naval blockade on Iran — as it happened
item.description: The Australian share market gained ground on Wednesday after weaker-than-expected US inflation figures boosted Wall Street and reduced the likelihood of the Federal Reserve lifting interest rates.

item.title: Tasmanian government has 'in-principle' support to explore purchasing Launceston's Boag's Brewery site
item.description: The Tasmanian government wants to buy Launceston's Boag's Brewery site and says it has "in-principle" support from the Japanese parent company to explore the idea. A developer says the site could be redeveloped into a mix of commercial properties, a boutique hotel, large public open spaces, offices and car parking.

item.title: Life at Davis Station: 24 minutes of sun after six weeks of darkness
item.description: Researchers spending winter at Australia's southernmost Antarctic research station gathered to celebrate the return of the Sun after weeks of darkness.

item.title: Man shot dead in Sydney's north-west in 'very brazen attack'
item.description: A man has been shot dead in Sydney's north-west, with the homicide squad now leading the investigation.

item.title: Aerial cull prompts push from sporting shooters to target feral deer in WA national parks
item.description: After a second annual aerial cull sparked controversy in southern WA, hunters say targeting feral deer from the ground is a more humane option.

item.title: Feral deer aerial cull.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Writer E Jean Carroll collects $8m in Trump sex abuse and defamation case
item.description: Writer E Jean Carroll has collected nearly $US5.6 million ($8 million) from Donald Trump after a jury in 2023 found the US president liable for sexually abusing and defaming her, court records show.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Spain's secret to success, Lamine Yamal's streak and Folarin Balogun's confession
item.description: Spain showcases its biggest advantage over France, Lamine Yamal's hot streak goes on and Folarin Balogun opens up on his overturned red card. Here are today's quick hits.

item.title: World Cup: France falters at first real hurdle against Spain as superstars fail to shine
item.description: France's 2026 World Cup campaign is ended by a poor 2-0 defeat to Spain, as the much-hyped collection of star forwards fail to turn up for the biggest game yet.

item.title: New political parties are exploiting Victoria's archaic electoral system
item.description: New political parties are seeking to exploit Victoria's anachronistic electoral system in the upper house to harvest voters' preferences and funnel them wherever they please.

item.title: Adelaide Crows player Josh Worrell to face court over Port Road crash
item.description: Adelaide Crows defender Josh Worrell has been reported by police and will face court over a traffic crash last week.

item.title: Scottish traffic cone gets formal Boston welcome after Scots fans' World Cup adventures
item.description: The World Cup's obsession with Scotland fans and traffic cones continues as a witch's hat from Glasgow receives an official welcome from dignitaries in Boston.

item.title: Flights bound for Adelaide cancelled and diverted due to thick fog
item.description: A number of flights have been delayed for take-off at Adelaide Airport due to heavy fog while others were either cancelled or diverted elsewhere.

item.title: Tyrannosaurus rex skeleton nicknamed 'Gus' sells for nearly $72m
item.description: One of the largest Tyrannosaurus rex skeletons discovered makes history as the most valuable dinosaur fossil bought at auction.

item.title: US uses explosive one-way drone boats for first time in combat against Iran
item.description: The United States is using one-way drone boats in combat against Iran for the first time in its military history. A defence expert says America is catching on to this "cheaper and more effective" tactic employed by Iran and Ukraine.

item.title: Kangaroo with joey shot with arrow at Lake Wallace
item.description: Community members search for the injured animal, which was spooked off before wildlife volunteers could help.

item.title: Moira Deeming drops court bid against party after last-ditch plea to retain candidacy
item.description: Victorian MP Moira Deeming faces being disendorsed as a Liberal candidate for refusing to apologise to former party leader Matthew Guy, after accusing him of assaulting her at a community event in May.

item.title: Death of man experiencing homelessness in winter prompts call for housing help
item.description: Millicent residents say they are concerned for the welfare of dozens of people sleeping rough in town.

item.title: Have you heard of vaginismus?
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Empty-nesters on how they 'fill the void' left by children
item.description: Experts say there can be unexpected grief for parents that comes from children leaving home.

item.title: Townsville gardener witnesses 'garbage guts' tree frog preying on sunbird
item.description: A Townsville gardener was watering her garden when she witnessed a white-lipped tree frog catch a sunbird mid-flight.

item.title: Mollie O'Callaghan reveals spinal stress fractures before Glasgow Commonwealth Games
item.description: Mollie O'Callaghan reveals she has defied doctors' diagnoses to swim at the Commonwealth Games and Pan Pacific Championships despite spinal fractures and a shoulder injury.

item.title: Timor-Leste scam centre compounds uncovered as police arrest Chinese, Indonesians, Cambodians
item.description: Police in Timor-Leste uncover a major international scam centre in the country, arresting more than 300 Chinese, Indonesian and Cambodian nationals in raids across the capital, Dili.

item.title: Boy, 5, dies after being hit by 4WD in Cloverdale, in Perth's south-east
item.description: A five-year-old boy dies and a 48-year-old woman is critically injured after being struck by a four-wheel drive outside a park in Cloverdale in Perth's south-east,

item.title: Man denied voluntary assisted dying wins partial victory in early test of ACT law
item.description: A tribunal has ruled on a man's bid to overturn his rejection from the ACT's assisted dying scheme in one of the first tests of how people can challenge such decisions.

item.title: The AFL is set on boutique stadiums for AFLW. Players think it is limiting the game
item.description: The AFL has introduced four double-headers this season, meaning games will be played at bigger venues, while the league remains steadfast on boutique grounds for standalone matches.

item.title: Adelaide Parklands works to continue despite parliamentary inquiry
item.description: Acting Premier Kyam Maher says works for the golf course redevelopment and MotoGP will continue despite a petition attracting 13,500 signatures.

item.title: Investigation into Sydney childcare worker expands to South Australian Indigenous community
item.description: A federal police investigation into an alleged childcare abuser has expanded to a remote Indigenous community in South Australia.

item.title: US claims to have struck military targets in Iran
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: The sledge between Diamonds teammates that helped break the ice after Super Netball Grand Final
item.description: Kate Moloney has had to put her team's Super Netball grand final nightmare aside to focus on the Commonwealth Games. Some gentle ribbing from her Aussie teammate has helped her see the lighter side.

item.title: US government claims operations have hit military targets in Iran
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Disgraced ex-footballer Nicky Winmar removed from AFL Hall of Fame
item.description: Winmar was found guilty earlier this month of three counts of unlawful assault and common law assault.

item.title: Pumped hydro and solar project approved for Gloucester, NSW
item.description: A pumped hydro project with the potential to power up to 120,000 homes has been given the green light in the Upper Hunter.

item.title: Basil Zempilas declares any Labor loss in Secret Harbour by-election 'a win for Liberals'
item.description: WA's Liberal leader says "there is no white flag being waved" in the upcoming Secret Harbour by-election, while indicating any result other than Labor retaining the seat, namely a win by One Nation, will be "a victory for the Liberals".

item.title: More than a quarter of Tasmanians will vote in new electorates, after AEC announces redistribution
item.description: More than a quarter of Tasmanians will vote in new electorates under a long-awaited boundary redistribution announced by the Australian Electoral Commission.

item.title: Tribunal told children had to use toilet in front of others in Cairns police watch house
item.description: Advocates for young people say conditions in a Queensland watch house have not improved since three teenagers' human rights were breached. A tribunal heard the boys had limited privacy and had to use the toilet in front of others.

item.title: Coronial inquest into mental health advocate's death recommends SA Police training changes
item.description: The coronial inquest into the death of William "Bill" Stockman did not confirm whether his death was preventable, but made multiple recommendations to the SA police minister.

item.title: Perth plumber restrains alleged car thief in viral video, but lawyer warns of risk
item.description: A Perth plumber has gone viral on social media after chasing down and restraining an alleged car thief after a botched getaway, but a prominent lawyer warns citizen's arrests are risky.

item.title: Labor aims to shape AI revolution with new data centre and AI copyright rules
item.description: The prime minister unveils Labor's plan for new AI national standards, warning Australia has a narrow window to set AI's "social licence".

item.title: What this World Cup semifinal means to England and Argentina
item.description: It is one of the biggest rivalries in men's World Cup history, and while the teams may downplay the history, England and Argentina fans are ready for another iconic showdown.

item.title: Daylight saving time could become permanent in the US. What's the situation in Australia?
item.description: If the bill becomes law, it will eliminate the need for states to change the clock twice a year.

item.title: Donald Trump's actions in Iran this week show the world he's run out of options
item.description: Donald Trump's actions this week have only confirmed he has run out of options and is now engaged in a simple war of wills, and calculations on who will run out of munitions first, with an enemy that he has underestimated from the start.

item.title: England v Argentina is a story of Maradona's Hand of God, Beckham's kick and Ramsey's 'animals'
item.description: England's World Cup semifinal clash against Argentina is the latest chapter in a sporting rivalry that dates back 60 years to an ill-tempered quarterfinal, featuring cheating, violence and redemption.

item.title: US global trade investigation targets Australian lamb imports
item.description: The United States International Trade Commission is investigating sheep meat imports, including from Australia, as American farmers struggle.

item.title: Darwin tobacconist avoids jail after being busted with 110,000 illegal cigarettes
item.description: A Darwin tobacconist has pleaded guilty to tax evasion after police busted him with more than 330 kilograms of tobacco labelled as Chinese tea and 110,000 illegally imported cigarettes.

item.title: Former assistant tax commissioner Nick Petroulias charged with fraud offences
item.description: Former assistant tax commissioner Nick Petroulias has been charged with multiple fraud offences, accused of obtaining financial advantage by deception and using false documents.

item.title: CDC races to track source of Cyclospora outbreak as cases spike in US
item.description: The US CDC is investigating thousands of possible cases across 34 states, as officials track down the source of the outbreak.

item.title: Trump reverses temporary pause on traffic stops after recent fatal shootings
item.description: US President Donald Trump says federal immigration agents will continue carrying out vehicle stops, reversing a temporary pause announced by officials just a day earlier.

item.title: New Zealand reports first case of H5N1 bird flu detected in brown skua seabird
item.description: The deadly virus was detected in a brown skua seabird near Wellington, although authorities say there is not yet any evidence of mass mortality or impacts on poultry.

item.title: Brisbane Broncos facing near-impossible task to reach NRL finals
item.description: Brisbane must reach 13 wins to have any chance of playing finals in 2026, but even that wasn't enough for them in 2022 and may not be again this year.

item.title: Wine Australia's National Vintage Report 2026 finds lowest harvest since 2000
item.description: Australian wine grape production has fallen to its lowest amount in more than 25 years as the industry adjusts to a combination of depressed market conditions.

item.title: LGBTQIA+ pop-up club operating in former Sydney church ordered to 'cease offensive trading'
item.description: The LGBTQIA+ venue says it is exploring its legal options after being issued with a breach of lease notice just days after opening at a former Sydney church.

item.title: Through my eyes: Daria Saville
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: 26 employees sue Meta over claims AI systems selected workers for lay-offs
item.description: Many of the employees in the lawsuit took pregnancy or parental leave, during which time they would not have worked and thus had their measured output reduced.

item.title: Pregnant NSW school worker accused of possessing child abuse material denied bail
item.description: The 35-year-old woman, who is seven months' pregnant, is in custody after police discovered alleged child abuse material on her mobile phone.

item.title: Alan Kohler Finance Report tumbnail
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Wednesday Finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Medical records and personal details stolen in GP network cyber attack
item.description: Partnered Health, which operates more than 50 general practice and skin cancer clinics across Australia, has identified more than a dozen clinics where it fears patient information was taken.

item.title: Boomanulla Oval in Narrabundah a step closer to Indigenous control
item.description: The transition of ownership is part of a government agreement with the ACT Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Elected Body.

item.title: A Perth plumber restrains an alleged car thief.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Oil reserves eased Iran war price shock, but now they are 'spent', IMF says
item.description: The International Monetary Fund says the global economy was spared the worst of the oil price shock because of large reserves. Now they are running out.

item.title: The future of AI
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: US launches new strikes at Iranian military targets in bid to secure Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US Central Command says it launched a 90-minute attack targeting Iran's strategic Greater Tunb Island.

item.title: The Odyssey may be the epic we deserve, but not the one we need
item.description: The scope of Christopher Nolan's latest epic might be awesome but a menagerie of supporting faces and questionable characterisations muddle the director's vision.

item.title: Argentina into World Cup final as England suffers heartbreaking collapse
item.description: Argentina has delivered England its latest World Cup heartbreak, scoring twice at the death to win 2-1 in a gripping semifinal in Atlanta.

item.title: I tried to use voodoo to win Race Around The World, and in some ways it worked
item.description: Halfway through the Race Around The World competition, I'm second last on the leaderboard. So, I decide to cheat.

item.title: Music quiz: Test your knowledge of instruments, musical terms and classical-inspired pop ballads
item.description: From famous icons and musical terms to unusual instruments and classical influences in popular music, test your knowledge with this quiz.

item.title: AI office urged to include artists after Anthony Albanese's copyright promise
item.description: The creative sector hopes there is a space to contribute to the Office of AI, given its content powers a lot of these tools.

item.title: Liberals canvas ideas to address gender, youth and 'deep-rooted' multicultural problem
item.description: Liberal Party HQ has admitted it is "in the political fight of our lives" as it canvasses ideas, including reviving gender quotas, to take the party forward.

item.title: Telstra and telco regulator prepare for grilling over widespread outage
item.description: Telstra prepares for a grilling in parliament about the recent network outage that disrupted Australia, with the communications regulator also set to face tough questions.

item.title: Why Australians shouldn't panic about latest surge in oil prices and state of supply
item.description: With the price of oil rising and the fuel excise cut due to end, drivers are facing twin threats when it comes to the price of fuel, but experts say Australia is in a much better position compared with when the Middle East conflict began.

item.title: Businesses globally claw back $110 billion after Trump's tariffs deemed unlawful
item.description: Businesses globally are clawing back tens of billions of dollars paid to United States customs after Donald Trump's so-called liberation day tariffs were deemed unlawful.

item.title: Albanese welcomes AI companies to Australia on our terms. We just don't know what those are yet
item.description: If Australia is truly going to coax major AI companies to choose us rather than the other suitor nations with similar ambitions, some tough decisions need to be made soon.

item.title: Chancellor says universities hide behind freedom of speech to avoid taking action on hate speech
item.description: The chancellor of Western Sydney University says tertiary institutions have hidden behind freedom of expression to avoid taking action on hate speech.

item.title: Tasmania's electoral boundary shakeup leaves politicians deciding where to run
item.description: Some notable politicians are among the 114,000 Tasmanians displaced by electoral boundary changes, and now have a difficult decision to make — stick with the electorate that voted them in, or move with the communities they've long been aligned with and live in.

item.title: Original architect of NDIS says scheme 'failing' to protect people it was created to help
item.description: Experts say the National Disability Insurance Scheme is failing people like Lucy, whose one-on-one care for severe disability has been deemed "not value for money".

item.title: Grant Whelan, victim of violent robbery on Tasmania's east coast, describes attackers as 'pure evil'
item.description: The 76-year-old victim of a violent robbery on Tasmania's east coast has described the people who attacked him as "pure evil".

item.title: Man found dead near property fire in Glenmore Park in Sydney's west
item.description: Police investigate the suspicious death of a man who was found in front of a partially alight property in Sydney's west.

item.title: AFP Commissioner Krissy Barrett and predecessor to be called in Masood Zakaria deportation case
item.description: Lawyers for accused underworld figure Masood Zakaria want to call AFP Commissioner Krissy Barrett to give evidence as he seeks to have his murder charges thrown out of court.

item.title: Calls for domestic violence disclosure scheme as regional family abuse rates rise
item.description: A survivor of family violence wants victims to have access to information about a person's previous offences.

item.title: Laser technology developed to test alcoholic drinks for methanol
item.description: Adelaide University researchers have developed a way to use lasers to test closed alcohol bottles for methanol, which kills hundreds of people every year.

item.title: Norway's Soeren Waerenskjold wins fastest-ever Tour de France stage as Tadej Pogačar protects lead
item.description: Tadej Pogačar protects his lead as Norway's Soeren Waerenskjold claims the fastest-ever stage in Tour de France history.

item.title: Peregrine falcon carer prepares for avian influenza
item.description: Peregrine falcons and wedge-tailed eagles could be among the first native species to contract a strain of bird flu that has recently arrived in Australia.

item.title: Wildfires across Canada's Ontario force evacuations and trap train drivers
item.description: Train operators in rural Canada were trapped by raging wildfires as nearby communities were evacuated.

item.title: Wildfires surround freight train near Armstrong, Ontario
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Former PAWS Darwin employees speak out about $150,000 in unpaid superannuation
item.description: Former staff of charity PAWS Darwin are speaking out about being short-changed on their superannuation to the tune of $150,000. Under-18 staff also say the super accounts PAWS promised to create for them never existed.

item.title: Scientists hope bones in underwater 'time capsules' will unlock more prehistory
item.description: Scientists who have been finding fossils in SA's underwater caves are hoping they can give a window into environmental changes.

item.title: Live: Minister accuses Coalition of being 'juvenile' for AI office criticism
item.description: Tim Ayres has accused the Coalition of being "juvenile" for criticising the establishment of an Office of AI in the prime minister's department. Follow live.

item.title: Six months after the alleged Invasion Day terror attack, inquiry hears stories of fear
item.description: A day of evidence reflecting on racism, hate and violence directed towards Aboriginal people highlights the ongoing impact of what police described as a potential "mass-casualty event" at a Perth rally.

item.title: Ancestral remains of Rapa Nui people begin long journey home after 143 years at Australian Museum
item.description: The remains of members of the Rapa Nui people from Easter Island are "finally going home" after sitting in the Australian Museum's collection for over a century.

item.title: Man dies and another seriously injured in e-scooter, e-skateboard crash
item.description: Police investigate an e-scooter and electric skateboard crash in Hervey Bay that has left one man dead and one seriously injured.

item.title: Live: ASX to rise as SpaceX shares see $1 trillion wiped
item.description: The Aussie share market is set to rise as SpaceX shares see more than $1 trillion dollars wiped. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

item.title: DV advocates warn AusAlert system could put regional women at increased risk
item.description: Regional women experiencing domestic violence are at high risk of their safe phones being discovered during tests of the new AusAlert disaster warning system.

item.title: Images of clothing released in bid to identify victims of alleged childcare abuser at Sydney centres
item.description: Images of distinctive clothing have been released as authorities attempt to identify victims of a Sydney childcare worker who allegedly abused more than 130 children.

item.title: Lionel Messi's World Cup magic inspires Argentina as history beckons in final against Spain
item.description: With England leading 1-0 and Argentina's hopes of back-to-back World Cup titles fading, superstar Lionel Messi once again becomes the hero.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: England boss Thomas Tuchel's tactics allow Lionel Messi to orchestrate Argentina win
item.description: England's World Cup ends in heartbreak again, with questions being asked of its manager's decisions as Lionel Messi plotted Argentina's win and the Three Lions' demise. Here are the quick hits.
